message:
Return NaN from nanmin/nanmax on incompatible Scalar types

Previously the inner `(Some(a), b)` arm of nanmin/nanmax would silently
keep the previous running min/max whenever `to_f64()` failed on either
scalar (e.g. a Utf8 mixed with an Int64). That mirrored neither pandas
("TypeError" or propagated NaN for incompatible dtypes) nor our own
handling of the first-element case, making the outcome dependent on
the iteration order of the input vec.

Rewrite the arm as an explicit match so the behavior is:

- Both scalars convert to f64 and the new value is smaller/larger:
  update the running min/max.
- Both convert to f64 but the new value is not more extreme: keep the
  current extremum (unchanged behavior).
- Either conversion fails (mixed/unsupported types): short-circuit and
  return `Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN)` for the whole reduction.

This makes the function dtype-aware in a way that pandas consumers
expect: a mixed string+numeric column collapses to NaN instead of
returning an arbitrary numeric element.

Add two regression tests in the existing #[cfg(test)] module:

- `nanmin_nanmax_mixed_incompatible_types_returns_nan` covers
  Int64+Utf8 and Utf8+Float64 inputs in both orderings.
- `nanmin_nanmax_compatible_numeric_types_ok` pins the pandas-style
  Bool/Int64/Float64 coercion (Bool(true) = 1.0) so the new code path
  cannot regress the all-numeric case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
